records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Miscellaneous Narrations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2518 - Ja‘far ibn al-Qasim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
"At the top of every bridge, there is a devil. When you reach it, say: 'In the name of Allah (swt),' and it will depart from you."
records.arabicText: 2518 - وَ رَوَى جَعْفَرُ بْنُ اَلْقَاسِمِ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِنَّ عَلَى ذِرْوَةِ كُلِّ جِسْرٍ شَيْطَاناً فَإِذَا اِنْتَهَيْتَ إِلَيْهِ فَقُلْ بِسْمِ اَللَّهِ يَرْحَلْ عَنْكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2518 - Ja‘far ibn al-Qasim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "At the top of every bridge, there is a devil. When you reach it, say: 'In the name of Allah (swt),' and it will depart from you."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Miscellaneous Narrations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2519 - Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) said:
"I guarantee for anyone who sets out on a journey while wearing a turban wrapped under their chin three things: they will not encounter theft, drowning, or burning."
records.arabicText: 2519 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : «أَنَا ضَامِنٌ لِمَنْ خَرَجَ يُرِيدُ سَفَراً مُعْتَمّاً تَحْتَ حَنَكِهِ ثَلاَثاً أَلاَّ يُصِيبَهُ اَلسَّرَقُ وَ اَلْغَرَقُ وَ اَلْحَرَقُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2519 - Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I guarantee for anyone who sets out on a journey while wearing a turban wrapped under their chin three things: they will not encounter theft, drowning, or burning."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Growing Hair for Hajj and Umrah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2520 - Mu‘awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"The Hajj is in well-known months" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:197)—Shawwal, Dhu al-Qa‘dah, and Dhu al-Hijjah. Whoever intends to perform Hajj should let their hair grow from the sighting of the crescent of Dhu al-Qa‘dah. Whoever intends to perform ‘Umrah should let their hair grow for a month."
records.arabicText: 2520 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : « «۝ اَلْحَجُّ أَشْهُرٌ مَعْلُومٰاتٌ ۝» ، - شَوَّالٌ وَ ذُو اَلْقَعْدَةِ وَ ذُو اَلْحِجَّةِ وَ مَنْ أَرَادَ اَلْحَجَّ وَفَّرَ شَعْرَهُ إِذَا نَظَرَ إِلَى هِلاَلِ ذِي اَلْقَعْدَةِ وَ مَنْ أَرَادَ اَلْعُمْرَةَ وَفَّرَ شَعْرَهُ شَهْراً».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2520 - Mu‘awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Hajj is in well-known months" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:197)—Shawwal, Dhu al-Qa‘dah, and Dhu al-Hijjah. Whoever intends to perform Hajj should let their hair grow from the sighting of the crescent of Dhu al-Qa‘dah. Whoever intends to perform ‘Umrah should let their hair grow for a month."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Growing Hair for Hajj and Umrah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2521 - It is narrated from Sama‘ah, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about cupping and shaving the nape during the months of Hajj.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it, nor in using Nuura (depilatory cream) or a toothstick (siwak)."
records.arabicText: 2521 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ سَمَاعَةَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْحِجَامَةِ وَ حَلْقِ اَلْقَفَا فِي أَشْهُرِ اَلْحَجِّ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ وَ لاَ بَأْسَ بِالنُّورَةِ وَ اَلسِّوَاكِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2521 - It is narrated from Sama‘ah, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) about cupping and shaving the nape during the months of Hajj.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it, nor in using Nuura (depilatory cream) or a toothstick (siwak)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Appointed Times for Entering Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2522 - Ubaydullah ibn Ali al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"The state of Ihram (ritual consecration) begins at five designated locations, established by the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family). It is not proper for a pilgrim, whether performing Hajj or 'Umrah, to assume Ihram before or after these places.
-	For the people of Medina, it is Dhul-Hulayfah (the Mosque of the Tree), where he (the Prophet) used to pray and declare the Hajj obligatory. When he exited the mosque and reached the plain of Al-Bayda, near the first milestone, he entered Ihram.
-	For the people of Sham (Syria), it is Al-Juhfa.
-	For the people of Najd, it is Al-'Aqiq.
-	For the people of Ta'if, it is Qarn al-Manazil.
-	For the people of Yemen, it is Yalamlam.
No one should deviate from the places designated by the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family)."
records.arabicText: 2522 - رَوَى عُبَيْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلْإِحْرَامُ مِنْ مَوَاقِيتَ خَمْسَةٍ وَقَّتَهَا رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ لاَ يَنْبَغِي لِحَاجٍّ وَ لاَ مُعْتَمِرٍ أَنْ يُحْرِمَ قَبْلَهَا وَ لاَ بَعْدَهَا وَقَّتَ لِأَهْلِ اَلْمَدِينَةِ ذَا اَلْحُلَيْفَةِ وَ هُوَ مَسْجِدُ اَلشَّجَرَةِ كَانَ يُصَلِّي فِيهِ وَ يَفْرِضُ اَلْحَجَّ فَإِذَا خَرَجَ مِنَ اَلْمَسْجِدِ فَسَارَ وَ اِسْتَوَتْ بِهِ اَلْبَيْدَاءُ حِينَ يُحَاذِي اَلْمِيلَ اَلْأَوَّلَ أَحْرَمَ وَ وَقَّتَ لِأَهْلِ اَلشَّامِ اَلْجُحْفَةَ وَ وَقَّتَ لِأَهْلِ نَجْدٍ اَلْعَقِيقَ وَ وَقَّتَ لِأَهْلِ اَلطَّائِفِ قَرْنَ اَلْمَنَازِلِ وَ وَقَّتَ لِأَهْلِ اَلْيَمَنِ يَلَمْلَمَ وَ لاَ يَنْبَغِي لِأَحَدٍ أَنْ يَرْغَبَ عَنْ مَوَاقِيتِ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2522 - Ubaydullah ibn Ali al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The state of Ihram (ritual consecration) begins at five designated locations, established by the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family). It is not proper for a pilgrim, whether performing Hajj or 'Umrah, to assume Ihram before or after these places.
-	For the people of Medina, it is Dhul-Hulayfah (the Mosque of the Tree), where he (the Prophet) used to pray and declare the Hajj obligatory. When he exited the mosque and reached the plain of Al-Bayda, near the first milestone, he entered Ihram.
-	For the people of Sham (Syria), it is Al-Juhfa.
-	For the people of Najd, it is Al-'Aqiq.
-	For the people of Ta'if, it is Qarn al-Manazil.
-	For the people of Yemen, it is Yalamlam.
No one should deviate from the places designated by the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Appointed Times for Entering Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2523 - In a narration by Rifa‘ah ibn Musa from Abu Abdullah (as), he said:
"The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) designated Al-‘Aqiq as the miqat (station for assuming Ihram) for the people of Najd, and he said: 'It is the miqat for wherever the land rises towards Najd.' You are among them.
He also designated Al-Juhfa as the miqat for the people of Sham (Syria), and it is also called Mahya‘ah."
records.arabicText: 2523 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ رِفَاعَةَ بْنِ مُوسَى عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «وَقَّتَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلْعَقِيقَ لِأَهْلِ نَجْدٍ وَ قَالَ «هُوَ وَقْتٌ لِمَا أَنْجَدَتِ اَلْأَرْضُ » وَ أَنْتُمْ مِنْهُمْ وَ وَقَّتَ لِأَهْلِ اَلشَّامِ اَلْجُحْفَةَ وَ يُقَالُ لَهَا مَهْيَعَةُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2523 - In a narration by Rifa‘ah ibn Musa from Abu Abdullah (as), he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) designated Al-‘Aqiq as the miqat (station for assuming Ihram) for the people of Najd, and he said: 'It is the miqat for wherever the land rises towards Najd.' You are among them.
He also designated Al-Juhfa as the miqat for the people of Sham (Syria), and it is also called Mahya‘ah."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Appointed Times for Entering Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2524 - Mu‘awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"It suffices you, if you do not know the location of Al-‘Aqiq, to ask the people and the Bedouins about it."
records.arabicText: 2524 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «يُجْزِيكَ إِذَا لَمْ تَعْرِفِ اَلْعَقِيقَ أَنْ تَسْأَلَ اَلنَّاسَ وَ اَلْأَعْرَابَ عَنْ ذَلِكَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2524 - Mu‘awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It suffices you, if you do not know the location of Al-‘Aqiq, to ask the people and the Bedouins about it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Appointed Times for Entering Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2525 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The beginning of Al-‘Aqiq is Barid al-Ba‘th, which is a station before Barid Ghamrah."
records.arabicText: 2525 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «أَوَّلُ اَلْعَقِيقِ بَرِيدُ اَلْبَعْثِ وَ هُوَ بَرِيدٌ مِنْ دُونِ بَرِيدِ غَمْرَةَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2525 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The beginning of Al-‘Aqiq is Barid al-Ba‘th, which is a station before Barid Ghamrah."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Appointed Times for Entering Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2526 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) designated Al-‘Aqiq as the miqat for the people of Iraq. Its beginning is Al-Maslakhu, its middle is Ghamrah, and its end is Dhat Irq. The beginning (Al-Maslakhu) is the most virtuous."
records.arabicText: 2526 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «وَقَّتَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ ، لِأَهْلِ اَلْعِرَاقِ اَلْعَقِيقَ وَ أَوَّلُهُ اَلْمَسْلَخُ وَ وَسَطُهُ غَمْرَةُ وَ آخِرُهُ ذَاتُ عِرْقٍ وَ أَوَّلُهُ أَفْضَلُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2526 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) designated Al-‘Aqiq as the miqat for the people of Iraq. Its beginning is Al-Maslakhu, its middle is Ghamrah, and its end is Dhat Irq. The beginning (Al-Maslakhu) is the most virtuous."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Appointed Times for Entering Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2527 - Mu‘awiyah ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man from Medina who assumed Ihram from Al-Juhfa.
Imam (as) replied: "There is no harm."
records.arabicText: 2527 - وَ سَأَلَ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَهْلِ اَلْمَدِينَةِ أَحْرَمَ مِنَ اَلْجُحْفَةِ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2527 - Mu‘awiyah ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man from Medina who assumed Ihram from Al-Juhfa.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Appointed Times for Entering Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2528 - It is narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "In Kufa, it is narrated that Imam Ali (as) said: 'Part of the completion of your Hajj is assuming Ihram from the vicinity of your home.'"
Imam (as) replied: "Glory be to Allah (swt)! If it were as they say, the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) would not have enjoyed wearing his clothes until reaching the Tree (Dhul-Hulayfah)."
records.arabicText: 2528 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنَّا نُرْوَى بِالْكُوفَةِ أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ «إِنَّ مِنْ تَمَامِ حَجِّكَ إِحْرَامُكَ مِنْ دُوَيْرَةِ أَهْلِكَ» فَقَالَ «سُبْحَانَ اَللَّهِ لَوْ كَانَ كَمَا يَقُولُونَ لَمَا تَمَتَّعَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ بِثِيَابِهِ إِلَى اَلشَّجَرَةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2528 - It is narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "In Kufa, it is narrated that Imam Ali (as) said: 'Part of the completion of your Hajj is assuming Ihram from the vicinity of your home.'"
Imam (as) replied: "Glory be to Allah (swt)! If it were as they say, the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) would not have enjoyed wearing his clothes until reaching the Tree (Dhul-Hulayfah)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Appointed Times for Entering Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2529 - Maysar asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who assumed Ihram from Al-‘Aqiq and another who assumed Ihram from Kufa, saying:
"Which of them has performed a better act?"
Imam (as) replied: "O Maysar, is it better to pray the afternoon prayer as four units or as six units?"
I said: "To pray it as four units."
Imam (as) said: "Likewise, the practice of the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) is better than anything else."
records.arabicText: 2529 - وَ سَأَلَ مُيَسِّرٌ اَلصَّادِقَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَحْرَمَ مِنَ اَلْعَقِيقِ وَ آخَرَ أَحْرَمَ مِنَ اَلْكُوفَةِ أَيُّهُمَا أَفْضَلُ عَمَلاً فَقَالَ «يَا مُيَسِّرُ تُصَلِّي اَلْعَصْرَ أَرْبَعاً أَفْضَلُ أَوْ تُصَلِّيهَا سِتّاً» فَقُلْتُ أُصَلِّيهَا أَرْبَعاً قَالَ «فَكَذَلِكَ سُنَّةُ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ غَيْرِهَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2529 - Maysar asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who assumed Ihram from Al-‘Aqiq and another who assumed Ihram from Kufa, saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Which of them has performed a better act?"
Imam (as) replied: "O Maysar, is it better to pray the afternoon prayer as four units or as six units?"
I said: "To pray it as four units."
Imam (as) said: "Likewise, the practice of the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) is better than anything else."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Appointed Times for Entering Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2530 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a man whose home is beyond Al-Juhfa: "From where should he assume Ihram?"
Imam (as) replied: "From his home."
records.arabicText: 2530 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ مَنْزِلُهُ خَلْفَ اَلْجُحْفَةِ مِنْ أَيْنَ يُحْرِمُ قَالَ «مِنْ مَنْزِلِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2530 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a man whose home is beyond Al-Juhfa:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"From where should he assume Ihram?"
Imam (as) replied: "From his home."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Appointed Times for Entering Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2531 - In another narration:
"Whoever's home is within the boundaries between the miqats and Makkah must assume Ihram from their home."
records.arabicText: 2531 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : «مَنْ كَانَ مَنْزِلُهُ دُونَ اَلْمَوَاقِيتِ مَا بَيْنَهَا وَ بَيْنَ مَكَّةَ فَعَلَيْهِ أَنْ يُحْرِمَ مِنْ مَنْزِلِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2531 - In another narration:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever's home is within the boundaries between the miqats and Makkah must assume Ihram from their home."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Appointed Times for Entering Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2532 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Whoever resides in Medina intending to perform Hajj for a month or so, and then decides to depart on a route other than that of Medina, when they are opposite Al-Shajarah and Al-Bayda, a distance of six miles, they should assume Ihram from there."
records.arabicText: 2532 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «مَنْ أَقَامَ بِالْمَدِينَةِ وَ هُوَ يُرِيدُ اَلْحَجَّ شَهْراً أَوْ نَحْوَهُ ثُمَّ بَدَا لَهُ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ فِي غَيْرِ طَرِيقِ اَلْمَدِينَةِ فَإِذَا كَانَ حِذَاءَ اَلشَّجَرَةِ وَ اَلْبَيْدَاءِ مَسِيرَةَ سِتَّةِ أَمْيَالٍ فَلْيُحْرِمْ مِنْهَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2532 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever resides in Medina intending to perform Hajj for a month or so, and then decides to depart on a route other than that of Medina, when they are opposite Al-Shajarah and Al-Bayda, a distance of six miles, they should assume Ihram from there."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Preparing for Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2533 - Mu‘awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"When you reach Al-‘Aqiq coming from Iraq, or any of the designated miqats (stations for Ihram), and you intend to enter Ihram, if Allah (swt) wills, then remove the hair from your underarms, trim your nails, clean your private area, and trim your mustache. It does not matter in which order you begin these actions. Then use the siwak (tooth-stick), take a ritual bath (ghusl), and wear your two garments for Ihram. Let this preparation, if Allah (swt) wills, be completed by the time of midday (zawal).
If it is not completed by midday, there is no harm, but it is more beloved to me that it coincides with midday."
records.arabicText: 2533 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا اِنْتَهَيْتَ إِلَى اَلْعَقِيقِ مِنْ قِبَلِ اَلْعِرَاقِ أَوْ إِلَى وَقْتٍ مِنْ هَذِهِ اَلْمَوَاقِيتِ وَ أَنْتَ تُرِيدُ اَلْإِحْرَامَ إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ فَانْتِفْ إِبْطَيْكَ وَ قَلِّمْ أَظْفَارَكَ وَ اِطْلِ عَانَتَكَ وَ خُذْ مِنْ شَارِبِكَ وَ لاَ يَضُرُّكَ بِأَيِّ ذَلِكَ بَدَأْتَ ثُمَّ اِسْتَكْ وَ اِغْتَسِلْ وَ اِلْبَسْ ثَوْبَيْكَ وَ لْيَكُنْ فَرَاغُكَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى عِنْدَ زَوَالِ اَلشَّمْسِ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ ذَلِكَ عِنْدَ زَوَالِ اَلشَّمْسِ فَلاَ يَضُرُّكَ إِلاَّ أَنَّ ذَلِكَ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ عِنْدَ زَوَالِ اَلشَّمْسِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2533 - Mu‘awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When you reach Al-‘Aqiq coming from Iraq, or any of the designated miqats (stations for Ihram), and you intend to enter Ihram, if Allah (swt) wills, then remove the hair from your underarms, trim your nails, clean your private area, and trim your mustache. It does not matter in which order you begin these actions. Then use the siwak (tooth-stick), take a ritual bath (ghusl), and wear your two garments for Ihram. Let this preparation, if Allah (swt) wills, be completed by the time of midday (zawal).
If it is not completed by midday, there is no harm, but it is more beloved to me that it coincides with midday."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Preparing for Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2534 - Mu‘awiyah ibn Wahb narrated:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) in Medina about preparing for Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "Prepare yourself in Medina, take everything you need, and if you wish, perform ghusl (ritual bath). If you wish, you may continue wearing your shirt until you reach Masjid al-Shajarah."
records.arabicText: 2534 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ وَهْبٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ نَحْنُ بِالْمَدِينَةِ عَنِ اَلتَّهَيُّؤِ لِلْإِحْرَامِ فَقَالَ «اِطَّلِ بِالْمَدِينَةِ وَ تَجَهَّزْ بِكُلِّ مَا تُرِيدُ وَ اِغْتَسِلْ إِنْ شِئْتَ ، وَ إِنْ شِئْتَ اِسْتَمْتَعْتَ بِقَمِيصِكَ حَتَّى تَأْتِيَ مَسْجِدَ اَلشَّجَرَةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2534 - Mu‘awiyah ibn Wahb narrated:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) in Medina about preparing for Ihram.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Prepare yourself in Medina, take everything you need, and if you wish, perform ghusl (ritual bath). If you wish, you may continue wearing your shirt until you reach Masjid al-Shajarah."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Preparing for Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2535 - Mu‘awiyah ibn Ammar asked about a man who removes hair (yattili) six nights before reaching the miqat.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
He also asked about a man who removes hair seven or eight nights before reaching Makkah.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 2535 - وَ سَأَلَ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَطَّلِي قَبْلَ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ اَلْوَقْتُ بِسِتِّ لَيَالٍ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ» وَ سَأَلَهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَطَّلِي قَبْلَ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ مَكَّةَ بِسَبْعِ لَيَالٍ أَوْ ثَمَانِ لَيَالٍ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Preparing for Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2536 - Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
A man asked Abu Abdullah (as) in my presence: "If I remove hair (apply depilatory) for the first Ihram, what should I do for the subsequent depilation, and what is the minimum interval between the two?"
Imam (as) replied: "If there are two Fridays in between, a total of fifteen days, then remove hair again (apply the depilatory)."
records.arabicText: 2536 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : سَأَلَ رَجُلٌ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ أَنَا حَاضِرٌ فَقَالَ إِذَا اِطَّلَيْتُ لِلْإِحْرَامِ اَلْأَوَّلِ كَيْفَ لِي أَنْ أَصْنَعَ فِي اَلطَّلْيَةِ اَلْأَخِيرَةِ وَ كَمْ حَدُّ مَا بَيْنَهُمَا فَقَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ بَيْنَهُمَا جُمْعَتَانِ خَمْسَةَ عَشَرَ يَوْماً فَاطَّلِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2536 - Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
A man asked Abu Abdullah (as) in my presence: "If I remove hair (apply depilatory) for the first Ihram, what should I do for the subsequent depilation, and what is the minimum interval between the two?"
Imam (as) replied: "If there are two Fridays in between, a total of fifteen days, then remove hair again (apply the depilatory)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Preparing for Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2537 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, who said:
We, a group in Medina, sent a message to Abu Abdullah (as), informing him that we wanted to bid him farewell. Abu Abdullah (as) sent back a reply:
"Perform ghusl (ritual bath) in Medina, for I fear that water may be scarce for you at Dhul-Hulayfah. Perform ghusl in Medina, wear the clothes you intend to enter Ihram in, and then come to me individually or in pairs."
We gathered at his place, and Ibn Abi Ya‘fur asked him: "What do you say about using oil after the ghusl for Ihram?"
Imam (as) replied: "Whether before, after, or during (Ihram), there is no harm in it."
Then, Imam (as) brought a bottle of bān salīkhah (a type of oil) that was empty except for a small amount, and he instructed us to apply it.
When we were ready to leave, Imam (as) said:
"It is not necessary, but if you find water upon reaching Dhul-Hulayfah, you may perform ghusl again."
records.arabicText: 2537 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ قَالَ : أَرْسَلْنَا إِلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ نَحْنُ جَمَاعَةٌ بِالْمَدِينَةِ أَنَّا نُرِيدُ أَنْ نُوَدِّعَكَ فَأَرْسَلَ إِلَيْنَا أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «أَنِ اِغْتَسِلُوا بِالْمَدِينَةِ فَإِنِّي أَخَافُ أَنْ يَعِزَّ اَلْمَاءُ عَلَيْكُمْ بِذِي اَلْحُلَيْفَةِ فَاغْتَسِلُوا بِالْمَدِينَةِ وَ اِلْبَسُوا ثِيَابَكُمُ اَلَّتِي تُحْرِمُونَ فِيهَا ثُمَّ تَعَالَوْا فُرَادَى وَ مَثَانِيَ، » قَالَ فَاجْتَمَعْنَا عِنْدَهُ فَقَالَ لَهُ اِبْنُ أَبِي يَعْفُورٍ مَا تَقُولُ فِي دُهْنَةٍ بَعْدَ اَلْغُسْلِ لِلْإِحْرَامِ فَقَالَ «قَبْلُ وَ بَعْدُ وَ مَعَ لَيْسَ بِهِ بَأْسٌ» قَالَ ثُمَّ دَعَا بِقَارُورَةِ بَانٍ سَلِيخَةٍ لَيْسَ فِيهَا شَيْءٌ فَأَمَرَنَا فَادَّهَنَّا مِنْهَا فَلَمَّا أَرَدْنَا أَنْ نَخْرُجَ قَالَ «لاَ عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْ تَغْتَسِلُوا إِنْ وَجَدْتُمْ مَاءً إِذَا بَلَغْتُمْ ذَا اَلْحُلَيْفَةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2537 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, who said:
We, a group in Medina, sent a message to Abu Abdullah (as), informing him that we wanted to bid him farewell. Abu Abdullah (as) sent back a reply:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Perform ghusl (ritual bath) in Medina, for I fear that water may be scarce for you at Dhul-Hulayfah. Perform ghusl in Medina, wear the clothes you intend to enter Ihram in, and then come to me individually or in pairs."
We gathered at his place, and Ibn Abi Ya‘fur asked him: "What do you say about using oil after the ghusl for Ihram?"
Imam (as) replied: "Whether before, after, or during (Ihram), there is no harm in it."
Then, Imam (as) brought a bottle of bān salīkhah (a type of oil) that was empty except for a small amount, and he instructed us to apply it.
When we were ready to leave, Imam (as) said:
"It is not necessary, but if you find water upon reaching Dhul-Hulayfah, you may perform ghusl again."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Preparing for Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2538 - Muhammad al-Halabi asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the use of khayri (a type of oil derived from flowers) and banafsaj (violet oil) for anointing oneself when intending to enter Ihram.
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, it is permissible."
Muhammad then asked him about a person who performs ghusl (ritual bath) in Medina for their Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "It suffices them, and they do not need to perform another ghusl at Dhul-Hulayfah."
records.arabicText: 2538 - وَ سَأَلَهُ مُحَمَّدٌ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ : عَنْ دُهْنِ اَلْخِيرِيِّ وَ اَلْبَنَفْسَجِ أَنَدَّهِنُ بِهِ إِذَا أَرَدْنَا أَنْ نُحْرِمَ قَالَ «نَعَمْ» وَ سَأَلَهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَغْتَسِلُ بِالْمَدِينَةِ لِإِحْرَامِهِ فَقَالَ «يُجْزِيهِ ذَلِكَ مِنَ اَلْغُسْلِ بِذِي اَلْحُلَيْفَةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2538 - Muhammad al-Halabi asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the use of khayri (a type of oil derived from flowers) and banafsaj (violet oil) for anointing oneself when intending to enter Ihram.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Yes, it is permissible."
Muhammad then asked him about a person who performs ghusl (ritual bath) in Medina for their Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "It suffices them, and they do not need to perform another ghusl at Dhul-Hulayfah."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Preparing for Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2539 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Imam (as) that a person may anoint themselves with any oil they wish before performing ghusl for Ihram, provided that the oil does not contain:
-	Musk
-	Ambergris
-	Saffron
-	Wars (a type of yellow plant dye).
Imam (as) also added: "Do not perfume your Ihram garments with incense (tajmir)."
records.arabicText: 2539 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : اَلرَّجُلُ يَدَّهِنُ بِأَيِّ دُهْنٍ شَاءَ إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ فِيهِ مِسْكٌ وَ لاَ عَنْبَرٌ وَ لاَ زَعْفَرَانٌ وَ لاَ وَرْسٌ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَغْتَسِلَ لِلْإِحْرَامِ قَالَ «وَ لاَ تُجَمِّرْ ثَوْباً لِإِحْرَامِكَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2539 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Imam (as) that
records.thaqalaynMatn:
a person may anoint themselves with any oil they wish before performing ghusl for Ihram, provided that the oil does not contain:
-	Musk
-	Ambergris
-	Saffron
-	Wars (a type of yellow plant dye).
Imam (as) also added: "Do not perfume your Ihram garments with incense (tajmir)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Preparing for Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2540 - Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad al-Jawhari narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who anoints himself with an oil containing fragrance while intending to enter Ihram.
Imam (as) replied: "Do not anoint yourself when you intend to enter Ihram with oil containing musk or ambergris, as their scent may linger in your hair after entering Ihram. However, you may use any other type of oil when preparing for Ihram, either before or after ghusl. Once you have entered Ihram, using any oil becomes forbidden until you complete the rites and exit Ihram."
records.arabicText: 2540 - وَ رَوَى اَلْقَاسِمُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ اَلْجَوْهَرِيُّ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَدَّهِنُ بِدُهْنٍ فِيهِ طِيبٌ وَ هُوَ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يُحْرِمَ فَقَالَ «لاَ تَدَّهِنْ حِينَ تُرِيدُ أَنْ تُحْرِمَ بِدُهْنٍ فِيهِ مِسْكٌ وَ لاَ عَنْبَرٌ يَبْقَى رِيحُهُ فِي رَأْسِكَ بَعْدَ مَا تُحْرِمُ وَ اِدَّهِنْ بِمَا شِئْتَ مِنَ اَلدُّهْنِ حِينَ تُرِيدُ أَنْ تُحْرِمَ قَبْلَ اَلْغُسْلِ وَ بَعْدَهُ فَإِذَا أَحْرَمْتَ فَقَدْ حَرُمَ عَلَيْكَ اَلدُّهْنُ حَتَّى تُحِلَّ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2540 - Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad al-Jawhari narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who anoints himself with an oil containing fragrance while intending to enter Ihram.
Imam (as) replied: "Do not anoint yourself when you intend to enter Ihram with oil containing musk or ambergris, as their scent may linger in your hair after entering Ihram. However, you may use any other type of oil when preparing for Ihram, either before or after ghusl. Once you have entered Ihram, using any oil becomes forbidden until you complete the rites and exit Ihram."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Preparing for Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2541 - Hammad narrated from Hariz, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
Imam (as) saw no harm if a woman applied kohl, anointed herself with oil, and performed ghusl after doing all of this in preparation for Ihram.
records.arabicText: 2541 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنْ حَرِيزٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ كَانَ لاَ يَرَى بَأْساً بِأَنْ تَكْتَحِلَ اَلْمَرْأَةُ وَ تَدَّهِنَ وَ تَغْتَسِلَ بَعْدَ هَذَا كُلِّهِ لِلْإِحْرَامِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2541 - Hammad narrated from Hariz, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) saw no harm if a woman applied kohl, anointed herself with oil, and performed ghusl after doing all of this in preparation for Ihram.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Preparing for Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2542 - In a narration by Jamil, Imam (as) said:
"The ghusl of your day suffices for your night, and the ghusl of your night suffices for your day."
records.arabicText: 2542 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ جَمِيلٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «غُسْلُ يَوْمِكَ يُجْزِيكَ لِلَيْلَتِكَ وَ غُسْلُ لَيْلَتِكَ يُجْزِيكَ لِيَوْمِكَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2542 - In a narration by Jamil, Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The ghusl of your day suffices for your night, and the ghusl of your night suffices for your day."
